description: Identifies Lambda cost inefficiencies from memory over-allocation, timeout misconfiguration, provisioned concurrency, ephemeral storage, architecture choice, and retry/error behavior.

cost.pricing_source: Static fallback estimate. Prefer AWS Pricing MCP, AWS Pricing API, or cost_report region-specific pricing when available.
cost.note: Lambda duration is billed by allocated memory and duration. Memory also changes CPU/network allocation, so lower memory can increase duration and reduce or erase savings.

severity_rules.name: memory_rightsize_candidate
severity_rules.condition: avg(max_memory_used_mb / memory_size_mb) < memory_utilization_ratio_avg_max AND max_memory_used_mb / memory_size_mb < memory_utilization_ratio_max_max AND modeled_lower_memory_total_cost < current_cost AND errors == 0 AND throttles == 0
severity_rules.description: Memory may be over-allocated, but change only when p95/p99 duration and modeled lower-memory cost show savings without latency, error, or throttle risk.

severity_rules.name: timeout_review_candidate
severity_rules.condition: timeout_sec >= timeout_reduce_threshold_sec AND duration_ms_p99 < timeout_sec * 1000 / timeout_recommended_p99_multiplier
severity_rules.description: Timeout is much larger than p99 duration. Reduce only after checking max duration, upstream/downstream timeout contracts, retries, and batch/stream processing needs.

severity_rules.name: provisioned_concurrency_underutilized
severity_rules.description: Provisioned concurrency appears underutilized. Tune schedules or configured concurrency after checking latency SLA and cold-start requirements.

severity_rules.name: ephemeral_storage_overallocated
severity_rules.description: Ephemeral storage above the free baseline may add cost. Reduce only when workload storage usage is known.

severity_rules.name: arm64_model_candidate
severity_rules.condition: architecture == x86_64 AND dependencies_support_arm64 == true AND cost_model_arm64_lower == true
severity_rules.description: arm64 may be cheaper for compatible workloads. Benchmark and validate dependencies before switching.

severity_rules.name: error_retry_cost_driver
severity_rules.condition: errors > 0 OR timeouts > 0 OR retries_high == true OR event_source_backlog_high == true
severity_rules.description: Errors, timeouts, retries, or backlog may be driving avoidable Lambda and downstream cost. Fix reliability before pure rightsizing.

severity_rules.name: downstream_request_amplification
severity_rules.condition: downstream_requests_per_invocation is high_or_rising AND cache_hit_rate is missing_or_low AND downstream_request_cost_material == true
severity_rules.description: Repeated downstream storage/API calls can drive both Lambda duration and request spend. Attribute the caller and reduce calls or add caching before treating the issue as memory-only waste.

memory_recommendation.note: Recommended memory should be the smallest valid value >= max_memory_used_mb * headroom_multiplier that still meets p95/p99 latency and total cost goals. Lambda supports 128 MB to 10,240 MB in 1 MB increments.
prefer_power_tuning_when: duration at target memory is unknown
monthly cost is high enough to justify an experiment

timeout_recommendation.note: Recommended timeout is usually ceil(duration_ms_p99 / 1000 * timeout_recommended_p99_multiplier), bounded by upstream/downstream contracts and a minimum timeout.

validation_before_change: Check p95/p99/max duration, max memory used, errors, throttles, timeouts, and concurrency.
Model duration change before reducing memory.
Check event source retry and batch settings.
Check provisioned concurrency utilization and schedules.
reporting: Separate compute, request, provisioned concurrency, ephemeral storage, architecture, and retry/error savings.
Use architecture-specific and region-specific pricing when available.
Report confidence based on metric coverage and cost model quality.
